name: Crockpot Rice Pudding with Fruit
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup dried apricots OR peaches, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Use half nonfat and half whole milk, or all nonfat for lower fat content.
2. Substitute canned milk for the regular milk for a very rich flavor.
3. The cooking time will vary greatly, anywhere from 1-1/2 to 3-1/2 hours. The longer it cooks, the thicker it will be.
4. It is important to have the dried apricots minced. Put all ingredients into crockpot.
5. Stir to blend well and cover.
6. Cook on high for 1-1/2 hours, stirring once after about 1 hour. Alternatively, cook on high for the first 30 minutes, then turn to low and cook as long as desired.
7. Check after the first 2 hours of low cooking and stir. If rice is not absorbing the milk quickly enough, turn the crockpot back up to high.
8. Keep cover on at all times. Crockpot temperatures vary widely among different brands; only experimentation can tell you the correct amount of time for cooking in your crockpot. Rarely will a crockpot recipe fail, though, as the long, slow cooking process does not require precise timing.
